patchNotes.headline: HTTPS, smoother voice, and sharper screen sharing
patchNotes.summary: v1.0.41 hardens transport security, removes duplicate-name suffixing, fixes room key recovery, upgrades voice and screen sharing quality, and now bridges older Mac installs forward from the legacy HTTP server onto the HTTPS production host after restart.

sections.title: Transport and identity
items: Server deployments can now run over HTTPS with native TLS support and optional HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect handling.
Older Mac installs can now download this release from the legacy HTTP production server and are automatically switched to the HTTPS production host after restart.
Profile names are no longer forced to be unique. Your npub stays the real identity, so multiple people can choose the same display name.

sections.title: Security and reliability
items: Private-key and signer sessions are now session-only and are no longer stored in the OS keychain.
Room key recovery is more resilient, which fixes the Windows "message could not be decrypted" issue when opening General chat.
Secure startup now clears stale session-only auth on restart instead of trapping you behind a blocked crypto screen.

sections.title: Voice and streaming
items: Voice channel responsiveness tuning from this pass is now the gold standard baseline for Mac performance.
Mic test playback is more reliable when output devices change or a saved sink disappears.
Screen sharing now targets 1080p at 30fps with higher bitrate headroom, better text and detail encoding hints, and improved mediasoup outgoing bitrate defaults.
Windows stays pinned to v1.0.40 until a real Windows-native build is published.
